description: Scrum Vocabulary Redesign — align takt's language to standard Scrum terms and introduce full Epic → Feature → Sprint skill hierarchy

userStories.title: Rename stories.json to sprint.json across all prompt files
userStories.description: As a takt user, I want the executable story file to be called sprint.json so that the terminology matches standard Scrum vocabulary.
acceptanceCriteria: All references to stories.json in run.md, worker.md, verifier.md, debug.md, retro.md, and reviewer.md are replaced with sprint.json
CLAUDE.md, README.md, and CHANGELOG.md reference sprint.json instead of stories.json
install.sh requires no changes to handle sprint.json (workers write the file directly — no install path needed)

userStories.title: Rename /takt-prd → /feature and /takt converter → /sprint
userStories.description: As a takt user, I want slash commands to use Scrum names so that /feature creates a Feature doc and /sprint converts it to an executable sprint.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/takt-prd.md is renamed to commands/feature.md with all internal vocabulary updated (PRD → Feature, stories.json → sprint.json, /takt-prd → /feature)
commands/takt.md (PRD converter) is renamed to commands/sprint.md with all internal vocabulary updated (PRD → Feature, stories.json → sprint.json, output path tasks/prd-*.md → tasks/feature-*.md)
install.sh removes old installed files (~/.claude/commands/takt-prd.md) and installs the renamed files correctly

userStories.title: Create /epic skill
userStories.description: As a takt user, I want a /epic skill that guides me through defining a high-level Epic before breaking it into Features, so that I have a structured starting point for large pieces of work.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/epic.md exists and conducts a guided interview (problem, users, goals, constraints) that produces tasks/epic-*.md with: problem statement, target users, goals, constraints, and proposed Feature breakdown with sequencing rationale
When CLAUDE.md is missing or thin, the skill presents an AskUserQuestion offering: (a) user describes the project, or (b) agent runs a quick codebase scan first — before starting the interview
At the end of the interview the skill asks if the user wants to proceed to /feature

userStories.title: Create /takt wrapper entry point
userStories.description: As a takt user, I want to type /takt to start the full planning flow from scratch, so that I have a single entry point regardless of where I am in the process.
acceptanceCriteria: commands/takt.md exists as a wrapper that guides through the full flow: Epic → Feature → Sprint → start takt, with a confirmation gate at each stage transition
The skill detects existing artifacts (tasks/epic-*.md, tasks/feature-*.md, sprint.json) and offers to skip ahead to the relevant stage
install.sh installs commands/takt.md to ~/.claude/commands/takt.md (replacing the old PRD converter that is now commands/sprint.md)
